---
title: "Simulation Diagnostics: Correlation, Relative RMSE, and Bias"
output:
  pdf_document:
    latex_engine: xelatex
geometry: margin=0.6in, landscape
fontsize: 12pt
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.width = 12,
  fig.height = 8
)

library(tidyverse)
library(reshape2)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
source("src/functions.R")
all_combinedTRUE <- readRDS( paste0("output/all_combined_",TRUE,".RDS")) %>% mutate(kind = "Sparse")
all_combinedFALSE <- readRDS( paste0("output/all_combined_",FALSE,".RDS")) %>% mutate(kind = "Not Sparse")
all_combined <- rbind(all_combinedTRUE, all_combinedFALSE)


results_combinedTRUE <- readRDS( paste0("output/results_combined_",TRUE,".RDS")) %>% mutate(kind = "Sparse")
results_combinedFALSE <- readRDS( paste0("output/results_combined_",FALSE,".RDS")) %>% mutate(kind = "Not Sparse")
results_combined <- rbind(results_combinedTRUE, results_combinedFALSE)


results_combined <- results_combined %>% 
  select(n, data, sigma,a,kind, rep, bart_uncali_mse,bart_tuned_mse, nn2_mse, rf_uncali_mse, probit_mse, bprobit_mse,
         bart_uncali_bias,bart_tuned_bias, nn2_bias, rf_uncali_bias, probit_bias, bprobit_bias,
         bart_uncali_bias_mn,bart_tuned_bias_mn, nn2_bias_mn, rf_uncali_bias_mn, probit_bias_mn, bprobit_bias_mn,
         probcor_bart,probcor_bart_tuned, probcor_nn, probcor_rf, probcor_probit, probcor_bprobit,
         mean_true, mean_bart, mean_probit, mean_bprobit, mean_bart_tuned, mean_nn, mean_rf,
         ) %>% 
  mutate(data = factor(data, levels = c("normal", "friedman", "step", "bin"),
                       labels = c("Linear", "Friedman", "Step", "Binary"))
  ) 



# Assumes these objects/functions already exist in your environment:
# - results_combined
# - cor_plot()
# - rmse_plot()   # should return relative RMSE (RMSE / mean_true) if you modified it
# - bias_plot()

# Helper for consistent, clear titles
plot_title <- function(plot_type, k, sig) {
  sparsity_txt <- ifelse(k == "Sparse",
                         "Sparse coefficient vector",
                         "Not sparse (full) coefficient vector")
  paste0( " sigma = ", sig, ", ", sparsity_txt)
}

# Helper to print plot + force a page break after it
print_plot_page <- function(p) {
  print(p)
  cat("\\newpage\n")
}


```

# Correlation plots

```{r cor_plots}
for (k in c("Sparse", "Not Sparse")) {
  for (sig in c(7, 15)) {
    p <- cor_plot(results_combined, k = k, sig = sig) +
      labs(title = plot_title("Correlation plot", k, sig))
    print_plot_page(p)
  }
}
```

# Relative RMSE plots (RMSE / mean(true)) as percent

```{r rmse_plots}
for (k in c("Sparse", "Not Sparse")) {
  for (sig in c(7, 15)) {
    p <- rmse_plot(results_combined, k = k, sig = sig) +
      labs(title = plot_title("Relative RMSE (RMSE / mean(true))", k, sig)) +
      scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1))
    print_plot_page(p)
  }
}
```

# Bias plots

```{r bias_plots}
for (k in c("Sparse", "Not Sparse")) {
  for (sig in c(7, 15)) {
    p <- bias_plot(results_combined, k = k, sig = sig) +
      labs(title = plot_title("Bias (mean absolute error in mean WTP)", k, sig))
    print_plot_page(p)
  }
}
```
